subject: Chemistry
title: What Comes After 118?
description: A fast breakdown of What Comes After 118? for builders.

hook.text: Element 119 would start row 8 of the periodic table. Nobody has managed to make it yet.

buildup.text: Japan's RIKEN lab has been trying since 2018, firing titanium beams at curium targets nonstop.

discovery.text: At element 120+, Einstein's relativity warps electron orbits so much that chemistry itself changes.

twist.text: Some theorists predict the table might end around element 172. Beyond that, atoms can't hold together.

climax.text: Finding 119 would prove we can keep pushing nature's limits. The race continues.

punchline.text: Row 8 awaits. The table's next chapter is unwritten.

quiz.question: What makes elements beyond 120 fundamentally different?
options: Relativistic effects warp electron behavior so much that chemistry changes
They would be too large to fit in any container
They would be lighter than hydrogen
